item.title: This is the vault at the end of the world where all the seeds on the planet are stored. It is located in the Svalbard archipelago, Norway, about 1,300 kilometers from the North Pole. Opened in 2008, this underground storage facility is designed
item.description: This is the vault at the end of the world where all the seeds on the planet are stored. It is located in the Svalbard archipelago, Norway, about 1,300 kilometers from the North Pole. Opened in 2008, this underground storage facility is designed to preserve 4.5 million seeds of crop plants from around the world, acting as insurance against the loss of genetic biodiversity caused by natural disasters, conflicts and climate change. So, in case the world is ever destroyed and you are the only survivor, you know where to go.

item.title: The forgotten programmers of the ENIAC
item.description: The ENIAC was the first programmable, electronic, general-purpose digital computer, completed in 1945. The ENIAC six: Kay McNulty, Betty Jennings, Betty Snyder, Marlyn Wescoff, Fran Bilas and Ruth Lichterman Not only did the women have to translate their calculations into steps that the ENIAC could handle, but they also had to literally wire the machine, said Kathy Kleiman, historian, author, and founder of the ENIAC Programmers Project, in a 2018 TEDx Talk. “They had to track each piece of data, wire it into a panel, such as a multiplier or a ‘square rooter,’ and then move the result — physically by wire — to another panel for storage,” she said. https://www.codecademy.com/resources/blog/eniac-six-women-programmed-computer/ Sauce https://www.instagram.com/share/reel/_m3L-X69Y (Part 2 of the video if you don't want to go to Instagram, but do it to promote that channel which is pretty great https://i.imgur.com/XslvrNK.mp4 )

item.dc:creator: Mirrormancer

item.title: Old misinformation is still misinformation!
item.description: The rumor that the library of Alexandria had burned down is an old and pervasive one. As is the idea that the library's destruction in any way represents knowledge lost. This is because of a fundamental misunderstanding of how the library functioned. It was not like a modern library, it was the royal library of the Ptolemaic Dynasty. By royal decree, all books that entered the city would be seized and added to the library's collection. As compensation, the library would produce a brand new, often higher-quality copy. This means that for every book that was in the library, there is at least one superior copy out there, somewhere. Even if the library had been destroyed physically, there would have been copies of most books in it out there, somewhere. Now on to the burning. There are many times that the library is rumored to have burned down, but the oldest version of the story remains the most persistent. While in Egypt during his last civil war, Julius Caesar set fire to a part of the city. A part nowhere near where the library is believed to have been, and which was quickly brought under control. The rumor that the library had been destroyed, then, was likely originally a piece of anti-caesar misinformation propaganda. If you would like reasons to hate Julius Caesar, then I recommend you read the book he wrote about the genocide he committed in Gaul which can be cited as a direct cause of France, so if you're anti-genocide or anti-french, you have good reason to despise Caesar. If you would like to mourn actual libraries that burned down, consider all the Mexican libraries destroyed by the Spanish, which has done irreversible damage to the Nahuatl language.
item.dc:creator: KalypsoKirin

item.title: Good on ya BC Hydro
item.description: https://financialpost.com/commodities/energy/electric-vehicles/bc-hydro-bans-tesla-charger-rebate-program For those of you who don't know, BC Hyrdo is a government owned utility. This basically cuts tesla out if the rebate program for the entire province of BC. This makes me proud to have been raised in BC. Well done.
